headline: 64-Year-Old Tech Exec Holds $1.6 Million in One Stock. The Wrong Move Could Cost $400,000.
summary: A 64-year-old software executive walks out of the office on her last day with $1.6 million sitting in a single employer stock. Her cost basis is $240,000, meaning roughly $1.36 million is embedded long-term capital gain waiting to be triggered. She has no W-2 income starting next January, a paid-off house, and a 401(k) she ... 64-Year-Old Tech Exec Holds $1.6 Million in One Stock. The Wrong Move Could Cost $400,000.
